{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:[[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]]

After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:[[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:[[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:[[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:[[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sano Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sano Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sano Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[en:Sano Shrine]]]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[festival|Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading {{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == {{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} |rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社[[ja:狭野神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading
