It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

revision.comment: 104 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:狭野神社 up to rev 102409886
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年9月13日 (日) 02:04 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||石川県能美市にある神社|狭野神社 (能美市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 狭野神社 |画像 = [[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
狭野神社 |所在地 = [[宮崎県]][[西諸県郡]][[高原町]]大字浦牟田117 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 神日本磐余彦天皇 |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[官幣大社]]別宮
[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[孝昭天皇]]朝 |例祭 = [[10月23日]] |神事 = 苗代田祭
狭野神楽 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=狭野神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[宮崎県]][[西諸県郡]][[高原町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、後に[[官幣大社]][[宮崎神宮]]の別宮となった。現在は再び独立の神社となり、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]になっている。[[霧島六所権現]]の一社である。 == 祭神 == {{JIS2004|対象=節|説明=鸕鶿草葺不合尊の最初の「鸕鶿」}} 神日本磐余彦天皇（[[神武天皇]]）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[吾平津媛|吾平津媛命]]・[[ニニギ|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]]・[[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫尊]]・[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鸕鶿草葺不合尊]]・[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]を配祀する。社名は[[神武天皇]]の幼名「'''狭野尊'''」に因むものである。 == 歴史 == {{出典の明記| date = 2022年2月| section = 1}} [[孝昭天皇]]の時代、[[神武天皇]]が誕生した地（[[高千穂峰]]の麓の'''皇子原'''）に創建されたといわれている。また一説には、往古は霧島山上に鎮座していたが、[[霧島山]]の噴火の厄難をさけて一旦この地に遷座したとも伝えられる。その後、数度にわたる[[霧島山]]の噴火により社殿の焼失と遷座を繰り返しており、現在地に遷座したのは[[慶長]]15年（[[1610年]]）である{{Cite book|和書 |title=新装普及版 神社辞典 |date=1997-09-25日 |publisher=東京堂出版}}。 当神社が創建当時鎮座していた元宮の地には、現在境外[[末社]]の'''皇子原神社'''が鎮座している。ここは[[神武天皇]]生誕の地といわれており、神社背後の「'''産婆石'''」（うべし）付近で生誕されたと伝えられている{{要出典|date=2022年2月}}。また皇子原神社の鎮座地は'''皇子原古墳群'''でもあり、6基の[[古墳]]があり、皇子原神社は'''1号古墳'''の上に鎮座している{{要出典|date=2022年2月}}。[[古墳]]は5世紀後半から6世紀前半の西諸県地方特有の[[地下式横穴墓]]と考えられている{{要出典|date=2022年2月}}。 伝承では、当地は[[神武天皇]]（'''狭野尊'''）が幼年期を過ごされた所だといわれており、この地には皇子原、皇子滝、皇子川原、血捨ノ木、狭野渡、御池の皇子港などの地名が残っている。 [[1873年]]に[[県社]]に列格したが、[[1915年]]に[[官幣大社]][[宮崎神宮]]の別宮に指定された。[[1898年]]からは、[[高木兼寛]]が[[宮崎神宮]]の大造営と同時に、[[神武天皇]]ゆかりの狭野神社の社殿の改修も行った。[[第二次世界大戦|第二次大戦]]後に再び独立の神社となり、[[昭和51年]]（[[1976年]]）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に列格した{{Cite book|和書 |title=宮崎県神社誌 |date=9月30日 |year=1988 |publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}。 == 祭事 == *[[祈年祭]]・特殊神事「苗代田祭」（[[2月18日]]） *[[御田植祭]]（[[5月16日]]） *[[例祭]]（[[10月23日]]） *[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]） *狭野神楽（12月第1土曜日、元は旧暦10月16日） - 「高原の神舞（かんめ）」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == ・白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』東京堂出版、1997年 ・『宮崎県神社誌』宮崎県神社庁、1988年 == 外部リンク == *[https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ 狭野神社]（狭野神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]] [[Category:高原町の歴史]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]

The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:jawiki oversized to be reviewed]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:Ja import oversized]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[狭野神社]] to [[Sano Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年9月13日 (日) 02:04 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||石川県能美市にある神社|狭野神社 (能美市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 狭野神社 |画像 = [[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
狭野神社 |所在地 = [[宮崎県]][[西諸県郡]][[高原町]]大字浦牟田117 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 神日本磐余彦天皇 |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[官幣大社]]別宮
[[別表神社]] |創建 = 伝[[孝昭天皇]]朝 |例祭 = [[10月23日]] |神事 = 苗代田祭
狭野神楽 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=狭野神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[宮崎県]][[西諸県郡]][[高原町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、後に[[官幣大社]][[宮崎神宮]]の別宮となった。現在は再び独立の神社となり、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]になっている。[[霧島六所権現]]の一社である。 == 祭神 == {{JIS2004|対象=節|説明=鸕鶿草葺不合尊の最初の「鸕鶿」}} 神日本磐余彦天皇（[[神武天皇]]）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[吾平津媛|吾平津媛命]]・[[ニニギ|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫命]]・[[ホオリ|彦火々出見尊]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫尊]]・[[ウガヤフキアエズ|鸕鶿草葺不合尊]]・[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]を配祀する。社名は[[神武天皇]]の幼名「'''狭野尊'''」に因むものである。 == 歴史 == {{出典の明記| date = 2022年2月| section = 1}} [[孝昭天皇]]の時代、[[神武天皇]]が誕生した地（[[高千穂峰]]の麓の'''皇子原'''）に創建されたといわれている。また一説には、往古は霧島山上に鎮座していたが、[[霧島山]]の噴火の厄難をさけて一旦この地に遷座したとも伝えられる。その後、数度にわたる[[霧島山]]の噴火により社殿の焼失と遷座を繰り返しており、現在地に遷座したのは[[慶長]]15年（[[1610年]]）である{{Cite book|和書 |title=新装普及版 神社辞典 |date=1997-09-25日 |publisher=東京堂出版}}。 当神社が創建当時鎮座していた元宮の地には、現在境外[[末社]]の'''皇子原神社'''が鎮座している。ここは[[神武天皇]]生誕の地といわれており、神社背後の「'''産婆石'''」（うべし）付近で生誕されたと伝えられている{{要出典|date=2022年2月}}。また皇子原神社の鎮座地は'''皇子原古墳群'''でもあり、6基の[[古墳]]があり、皇子原神社は'''1号古墳'''の上に鎮座している{{要出典|date=2022年2月}}。[[古墳]]は5世紀後半から6世紀前半の西諸県地方特有の[[地下式横穴墓]]と考えられている{{要出典|date=2022年2月}}。 伝承では、当地は[[神武天皇]]（'''狭野尊'''）が幼年期を過ごされた所だといわれており、この地には皇子原、皇子滝、皇子川原、血捨ノ木、狭野渡、御池の皇子港などの地名が残っている。 [[1873年]]に[[県社]]に列格したが、[[1915年]]に[[官幣大社]][[宮崎神宮]]の別宮に指定された。[[1898年]]からは、[[高木兼寛]]が[[宮崎神宮]]の大造営と同時に、[[神武天皇]]ゆかりの狭野神社の社殿の改修も行った。[[第二次世界大戦|第二次大戦]]後に再び独立の神社となり、[[昭和51年]]（[[1976年]]）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に列格した{{Cite book|和書 |title=宮崎県神社誌 |date=9月30日 |year=1988 |publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}。 == 祭事 == *[[祈年祭]]・特殊神事「苗代田祭」（[[2月18日]]） *[[御田植祭]]（[[5月16日]]） *[[例祭]]（[[10月23日]]） *[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]） *狭野神楽（12月第1土曜日、元は旧暦10月16日） - 「高原の神舞（かんめ）」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == ・白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』東京堂出版、1997年 ・『宮崎県神社誌』宮崎県神社庁、1988年 == 外部リンク == *[https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ 狭野神社]（狭野神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} [[Category:宮崎県の神社]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]] [[Category:高原町の歴史]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}}

After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:Sano Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]]

Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaharu|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimorokata District|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Cite sources|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōshō|qq=}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaki Kanehiro|qq=}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}} |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takaharu|ja|高原町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaharu|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District|ja|西諸県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishimorokata District|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮崎県|ja|宮崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Special Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|ja|孝昭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōshō|qq=}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|ja|高木兼寛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaki Kanehiro|qq=}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]]

After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takaharu, Miyazaki|Takaharu]], [[:en:Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|Nishimorokata District]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]]

{{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|qq=}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=[[File:Sano Shrine Takaharu.jpg|260px]]
Sano Shrine |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takaharu, Miyazaki|Takaharu]], [[:en:Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|Nishimorokata District]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[en:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]] [[Category:高原町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]]

It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Shrines of Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takaharu, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]] [[Category:高原町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]]

It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]] [[Category:高原町の歴史]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|qq=}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|qq=}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sano Shrine]] [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:高原町の建築物]] [[Category:高原の Municipal History]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]]
