[[commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3472357]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|1=Sano Shrine (Nomi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571758}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=File:Tsuno Shrine 2009 001.JPG |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity=[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |prefecture={{ill|Takaharu|WD=Q1206617}} |coordinates={{Coord|31.908333|N|130.969722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takaharu, Miyazaki|Takaharu]], [[:en:Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|Nishimorokata District]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]]. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]], at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, [[:en:Takaki Kanehiro|Takaki Kanehiro]] oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3472357]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|1=Sano Shrine (Nomi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571758}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=File:Tsuno Shrine 2009 001.JPG |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |prefecture={{ill|Takaharu|WD=Q1206617}} |coordinates={{Coord|31.908333|N|130.969722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takaharu, Miyazaki|en|Takaharu, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206617|lt=Takaharu}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|en|Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|WD=Q1156846|lt=Nishimorokata District}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. [[co:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|1=Sano Shrine (Nomi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571758}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=File:Tsuno Shrine 2009 001.JPG |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |prefecture={{ill|Takaharu|WD=Q1206617}} |coordinates={{Coord|31.908333|N|130.969722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takaharu, Miyazaki|en|Takaharu, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206617|lt=Takaharu}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|en|Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|WD=Q1156846|lt=Nishimorokata District}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro|1=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro|1=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sano-jinja (Takaharu)]][[eu:Sano-jinja]][[fr:Sano-jinja]][[ja:狭野神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=September 13, 2015}} {{Otheruses||Shrine in Nomi, Ishikawa|{{ill|Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine (Nomi City)|1=Sano Shrine (Nomi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571758}}}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan |name=Sano Shrine |image=File:Tsuno Shrine 2009 001.JPG |location=Uramuta 117, Takaharu, Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki Prefecture |Position={{Coord|31|54|30.9|N|130|58|11.6|E|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} |Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Imperial Shrine's Separate Shrine, Special Shrine |founded=Traditionally during the reign of Emperor Kōshō |festival=October 23 |Rituals=Naeshirota festival
Sano Kagura |map={{location map|Japan Miyazaki Prefecture|label=Sano Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |prefecture={{ill|Takaharu|WD=Q1206617|1=Takaharu, Miyazaki}} |coordinates={{Coord|31.908333|N|130.969722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (狭野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takaharu, Miyazaki|en|Takaharu, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206617|lt=Takaharu|1=Takaharu, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|en|Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki|WD=Q1156846|lt=Nishimorokata District|1=Nishimorokata District, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro|1=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and later became a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine. It is now an independent shrine and is listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is one of the shrines of the {{ill|Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja|霧島六所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Rokushō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 霧島六社権現|1=Kirishima Rokusha Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660872}}. == Deities == {{JIS2004|target=section|description=First character of Uganomitama}} The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, along with {{ill|Ahiratsuhime|ja|吾平津媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦火瓊瓊杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}}, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火々出見尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, and {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}. The shrine's name comes from Emperor Jimmu's childhood name, "'''Sano no Mikoto'''". == History == {{Unreferenced|date=February 2022|section=1}} The shrine is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}, at the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu (at the base of {{ill|Mount Takachiho|ja|高千穂峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takachiho|1=Mount Takachiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514128}}, in a place called '''Osa no Hara'''){{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. It is also believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kirishima and was moved to avoid volcanic eruptions{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The current location has been used since Keicho 15 (1610){{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. The original site of the shrine now houses the outer shrine, '''Osa no Hara Shrine'''. This site is believed to be the birthplace of Emperor Jimmu and has a stone called "'''Ubeishi'''", believed to be associated with his birth{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. The area is also an ancient burial site, known as the '''Osa no Hara Kofun Group''', dating back to the late 5th to early 6th centuries{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. Tradition holds that the area is where Emperor Jimmu spent his childhood, and it contains various place names related to him such as Osa no Hara, Osa no Taki, Osa no Kawara, Chisatenoki, Sano Watari, and Miike no Osa no Minato{{Citation needed|date=February 2022}}. In 1873, the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1915, it was designated as a separate shrine of the {{ill|Imperial Shrine|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Shrine|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} Miyazaki Shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=新装普及版 神社辞典|date=September 25, 1997|publisher=東京堂出版}}. From 1898, {{ill|Takaki Kanehiro|en|Takaki Kanehiro|WD=Q438528|lt=Takaki Kanehiro|1=Takaki Kanehiro}} oversaw major renovations of the shrine{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. After World War II, the shrine became independent again and was listed as a special shrine in 1976{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=宮崎県神社誌|date=September 30, 1988|publisher=宮崎県神社庁}}. == festivals == *[[Kinensai]] and the special ritual "Naeshirota festival" (February 18) *{{ill|8=ja|9=御田植祭|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue-sai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (May 16) *[[Reisai]] (October 23) *[[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November 23) *'''Sano Kagura''' (first Saturday of December, originally on the 16th day of the 10th lunar month) - designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan. == References == == Further Reading == * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki (eds.), ''Shinshō Fukyūban Jinja Jiten'', Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1997. * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal'', Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Agency, 1988. == External Links == * [https://sanojinjya.web.fc2.com/ Sano Shrine Official Website] {{Beppyo Shrines}}{{Shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|狭野神社|version=102409886|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:狭野神社]] [[ja:Sano Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sano-jinja (Takaharu)}} [[eu:Sano-jinja]] [[fr:Sano-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q3472357}} [[Category:Architecture of Takaharu Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Takaharu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
